---
title: "Zero Trust Modernization Validation Suite"
subtitle: "Conformance tests and validation criteria for the zero-trust domain"
doc-type: domain-validation-suite
audience: [Customer, Assessor, Adapter Owner]
classification: Controlled
boundary: GCC-Moderate
domain: zero-trust
canon-source: "src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-008-zero-trust-identity.md"
canon-reference:
  - "docs/customer-documents/modernization-specs/zero-trust/zero-trust.qmd"
  - "docs/customer-documents/whitepapers/zero-trust-governance-whitepaper.qmd"
derived-from: "uiao canon"
---

# Zero Trust Modernization Validation Suite

## Scope

Conformance tests and validation criteria for the Zero Trust domain.
Pairs with the
[Zero Trust Modernization Spec](../../../modernization-specs/zero-trust/zero-trust.qmd).
The suite gates whether a ZT modernization program meets substrate
conformance — primary focus is identity-as-root-namespace posture,
decision-record emission, and ZTMM pillar coverage.

[IMAGE-01: Zero Trust validation suite diagram. Five vertical pillar columns labeled Identity, Devices, Networks, Applications & Workloads, Data. Each column shows a small conformance checkmark or gap badge for the substrate-baseline tests. Across the bottom, a horizontal "Identity-as-root-namespace conformance gate" runs through all five columns. To the right, a panel showing "Decision-record emission shape: provenance + zt_decision claim". Clean engineering blueprint style, dark navy (#0D1B2E) and teal (#1E8C8C) on white background. No photographs, purely diagrammatic.]

## Test categories

### 1. Identity-primacy conformance

Per [ADR-008](../../../../../src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-008-zero-trust-identity.md):

- Every ZT decision consumes an identity claim as authoritative
  input.
- Devices, addresses, workloads, and data classifications resolve via
  identity-plane objects, not parallel namespaces.
- PDPs (policy decision points) do not maintain parallel identity
  authority.

### 2. Decision-record schema

For each ZT decision emitted:

- `pdp_id`, `decision`, `identity_context`, `resource_scope`,
  `policy_refs`, `evaluation_timestamp` are all present.
- `decision` is one of `permit | deny | escalate`.
- `policy_refs` resolve to canonical policy IDs.
- Decision record carries the full provenance envelope.

### 3. Pillar coverage

The domain demonstrates substrate-anchored evidence for each ZTMM
pillar:

- **Identity** — identity-plane SSOT, identity-derived addressing.
- **Devices** — device claims via provenance envelope.
- **Networks** — overlay decisions consume identity claims.
- **Applications & Workloads** — adapter-mapped controls + KSI
  bindings.
- **Data** — provenance envelope on every data-related claim.

### 4. Drift detection coverage

- `DRIFT-IDENTITY` (P1) — issuer cannot resolve; halt + alert.
- `DRIFT-AUTHZ` (P1/P2) — decision outside consent envelope.
- `DRIFT-SEMANTIC` — pillar state stale (e.g. retired role still
  emitting decisions).
- `DRIFT-PROVENANCE` — cross-pillar correlation chain broken.

## Evidence expectations

- Per-decision claim records with full provenance.
- Cross-pillar correlation evidence (the same identity resolving
  identically across pillars).
- ZTMM dashboard derivations from canonical claims (no parallel
  attestation pipeline).
- OSCAL artifacts mapping ZT decisions onto NIST 800-207 and CISA
  ZTMM controls.

## Conformance gates

| Gate | Required | Tier |
|---|---|---|
| **T1 — identity primacy** | All ZT decisions consume identity-plane objects | Required |
| **T2 — decision schema** | Decision records validate against canon schema | Required |
| **T3 — pillar coverage** | Substrate-anchored evidence in each ZTMM pillar | Target |
| **T4 — drift coverage** | All four ZT-relevant drift classes detected | Target |

## Drift procedures

ZT operations focus on the P1/P2 envelope. P1 findings (identity
unresolvable, AuthZ envelope violation) halt the affected decision
flow and alert. P2 findings auto-remediate when deterministic;
otherwise escalate within 1 hour.

## Honest limits

- Optimal ZTMM maturity requires continuous, automated, cross-pillar
  policy evaluation. Substrate ships the canonical fabric; per-pillar
  runtime maturity varies.
- GCC-Moderate platform feature gaps reduce out-of-box ZT analytics
  fidelity; compensating-enforcement adapters are domain-validation
  responsibility, not the substrate's.
- T3 / T4 are target-state for most agencies today; T1 / T2 form the
  baseline conformance bar.

## Related documents

- [Modernization Spec — Zero Trust](../../../modernization-specs/zero-trust/zero-trust.qmd)
- [Whitepaper — Zero Trust Governance](../../../whitepapers/zero-trust-governance-whitepaper.qmd)
- [ADR-008: Zero Trust Identity](../../../../../src/uiao/canon/adr/adr-008-zero-trust-identity.md)
